<div style="--fade-in-animation: fade-in-new-response 150ms cubic-bezier(0.3,0,0.8,0.15); animation: auto ease 0s 1 normal none running none; padding-top: 30px; position: relative; opacity: 1;"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>how does blockchain work techinically</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div id="aim-chrome-initial-inline-async-container"><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Technically, a blockchain is a<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">decentralized, distributed ledger</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that records transactions across a network of computers in a way that is chronologically consistent and immutable. It functions by grouping data into "blocks," securing them with cryptography, and linking them together to form a permanent chain.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 82.1328px;"><img id="img-2b2-aezzJp_8ptQPlKq_iQk_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="TRM Labs" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">TRM Labs</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+3</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Core Technical Components<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Blocks</strong>: The basic building units that store a batch of valid transactions, a timestamp, and metadata.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Cryptographic Hashes</strong>: Each block contains a unique digital fingerprint (hash) of its own contents and the hash of the previous block. This link ensures that if any data in a previous block is altered, its hash changes, breaking the entire chain and making tampering immediately detectable.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Nodes</strong>: Individual computers in the network that each maintain a complete or partial copy of the ledger, ensuring there is no single point of failure.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Consensus Mechanism</strong>: A set of rules (algorithms) that nodes use to agree on the "true" state of the ledger without a central authority.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 144.969px;"><img id="img-2b2-aezzJp_8ptQPlKq_iQk_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Amazon Web Services" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Amazon Web Services</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+7</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The Transaction Process<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Initiation</strong>: A user creates a transaction and signs it with their<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">private key</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to prove ownership and authenticity.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Broadcasting</strong>: The transaction is broadcast to the network and enters a temporary waiting area called a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">mempool</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Verification</strong>: Nodes check the transaction against protocol rules, such as verifying digital signatures and ensuring the sender has a sufficient balance to prevent "double-spending".</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Block Creation</strong>: A specialized node (a miner or validator) selects transactions from the mempool and bundles them into a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">candidate block</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Validation (Consensus)</strong>: The network must approve the block through a consensus algorithm:</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Proof of Work (PoW)</strong>: Miners compete to solve a complex mathematical puzzle by guessing a "nonce" until the resulting block hash meets a specific difficulty target.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Proof of Stake (PoS)</strong>: Validators are selected to propose or approve blocks based on the amount of cryptocurrency they have "staked" as collateral.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Addition</strong>: Once validated, the new block is appended to the chain and broadcast to all other nodes, who update their local copies of the ledger.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 112.281px;"><img id="img-2b2-aezzJp_8ptQPlKq_iQk_18" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Stanford Online" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Stanford Online</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+9</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ol><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Security Features<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Immutability</strong>: Because each block references the previous one's hash, changing a single historical transaction would require re-calculating the hashes for that block and all subsequent blocks across the entire network—a task that is computationally impossible in large networks.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Transparency</strong>: In public blockchains, any participant can independently verify the transaction history, creating a "single source of truth".</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 82.25px;"><img id="img-2b2-aezzJp_8ptQPlKq_iQk_19" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="TRM Labs" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">TRM Labs</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Would you like to dive deeper into a specific<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">consensus algorithm</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or see how<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">smart contracts</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>fit into this architecture?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Yes, blockchains can work without a cryptocurrency</div>. While the two are often linked, cryptocurrency is simply a specific use case—a digital asset managed by the underlying blockchain technology.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 65.125px;"><img id="img-v8O-adP0H4rl5NoPiYPn4AM_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Reddit" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Reddit</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The necessity of a cryptocurrency depends largely on whether the blockchain is<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">public</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">private</strong>.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Public vs. Private Blockchains<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Public Blockchains (Need Crypto)</strong>: These are "permissionless" networks like Bitcoin or Ethereum. Because anyone can join and act as a validator (miner), the network must provide a financial incentive (cryptocurrency) to reward them for their work and electricity costs. Without this native token, there would be no reason for independent strangers to secure the network.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Private/Enterprise Blockchains (No Crypto Needed)</strong>: These are "permissioned" networks used by organizations, such as<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Hyperledger Fabric</span><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">R3 Corda</span>. In these systems, participants are known and authorized, and their incentive to maintain the ledger is often tied to business efficiency or contractual obligations rather than a digital coin.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 64.9141px;"><img id="img-v8O-adP0H4rl5NoPiYPn4AM_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Reddit" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Why You Might Remove the "Currency"<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Blockchains without a native currency are often referred to as<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT)</strong>. Technically, these systems function as high-security, shared databases for:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Supply Chain Tracking</strong>: Verifying the origin and movement of goods.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Healthcare Records</strong>: Securely sharing patient data between hospitals and insurers while maintaining privacy.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Voting Systems</strong>: Creating tamper-proof records for elections to prevent fraud.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Smart Contracts</strong>: Automating business agreements that execute only when certain conditions are met, without needing a third-party intermediary.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Blockchains defend against fake data attacks primarily through<span>&nbsp;</span></div><mark style="color: var(--jINu6c); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--jwFC2e); padding: 0px 2px; font-weight: 500;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">consensus mechanisms</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">deterministic verification</strong></mark>, which allow the network to reject incorrect information even if it comes from a malicious participant. In your specific example of finding the next biggest prime number, the network wouldn't just "take your word for it"; it would use several layers of technical defense.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 68px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Ledger" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Ledger</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. Deterministic Verification<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">For a task like prime number discovery, the answer is "mathematically objective." When a node claims to have found a new prime, every other node in the network can perform a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">primality test</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(like the<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11567616/" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">Miller-Rabin</a></span><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">AKS</span><span>&nbsp;</span>tests) to independently verify the result.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 194.305px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="National Institutes of Health (.gov)" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">National Institutes of Health (.gov)</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;"></span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Defense</strong>: If a malicious node submits a non-prime number, other nodes will run the verification code, see that it fails, and immediately discard the data before it ever reaches the ledger.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 77.3125px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_3" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="MoonPay" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">MoonPay</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. Byzantine Fault Tolerance (BFT)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Blockchains are designed to solve the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Byzantine Generals' Problem</strong>, which is the challenge of reaching agreement in a network where some participants might be lying.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 65.9453px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_4" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Ledger" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Ledger</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Defense</strong>: Most blockchains require a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">supermajority</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(usually 2/3 or more) of nodes to agree that a piece of data is valid before it is added. Even if a small group of "fake" nodes tries to push a wrong prime number, the honest majority will outvote them and maintain the correct record.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 57.6328px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_5" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="dYdX" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">dYdX</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+3</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">3. Economic Penalties (Slashing)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">In "Proof of Stake" systems, participants must lock up (stake) valuable assets to participate in verification.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 72.1797px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_21" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Consensys" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Consensys</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;"></span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Defense</strong>: If a node is caught submitting fake data (like a false prime), the network can "slash" their stake, causing them to<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">lose their money</strong>. This makes attacking the network with fake data extremely expensive and irrational for the attacker.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 129.156px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_22" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="ScienceDirect.com" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">ScienceDirect.com</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">4. Real-World Example: Primecoin<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">There is actually a blockchain called<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://bitcoinmagazine.com/business/primecoin-the-cryptocurrency-whose-mining-is-actually-useful-1373298534" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">Primecoin</a></span><span>&nbsp;</span>that uses the search for "prime chains" as its security mechanism.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 117.75px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_23" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Bitcoin Magazine" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Bitcoin Magazine</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works</strong>: Miners find specific sequences of prime numbers to secure the network.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Verification</strong>: Because it is much easier to<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">check</em><span>&nbsp;</span>if a number is prime than it is to<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">find</em><span>&nbsp;</span>it, any node can verify a miner's work in milliseconds, making "fake" prime submissions impossible to sustain.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 63.8516px;"><img id="img-NsS-abaxGPWkiLMPlKLf0Qo_24" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Substack" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Substack</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;"></span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Would you like to know more about how<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Zero-Knowledge Proofs</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>allow a node to prove a calculation is correct without revealing the work itself?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; height: fit-content; margin-left: 8px; margin-top: auto; display: flex; flex: 1 1 auto; place-content: end; outline: 0px;"><div style="color: rgb(139, 140, 145); height: 14px; margin-bottom: 2px; rotate: 180deg; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Blockchains defend against collusion (where multiple nodes "lie" together) by making it<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">economically or computationally ruinous</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to do so. In your example, if an attacker creates 1,000 "fake" nodes to all swear that<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">14</code><span>&nbsp;</span>is a prime number, they are attempting what is known as a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Sybil Attack</strong>.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. Defending Against Collusion (The "Prime Test" Attack)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">The network doesn't just count the number of "votes"; it weights them based on a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Consensus Mechanism</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that requires a real-world sacrifice:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 99.5859px;"><img id="img-ncS-afbEMPuy5NoPyqXZqAI_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Blockdaemon" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Blockdaemon</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Proof of Work (PoW)</strong>: In a PoW system (like Bitcoin), voting power is tied to physical hardware and electricity. To "fake" a result, an attacker must control more than 50% of the entire network's total computing power (a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">51% Attack</strong>). Even if they have 1,000 fake identities, they still only have the same limited amount of hardware to solve the puzzle, making it almost impossible to out-compete the rest of the world.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Proof of Stake (PoS)</strong>: In a PoS system (like Ethereum), voting power is tied to how much of the network’s currency a node "stakes" as collateral. If a group of nodes colludes to pass fake data, the network can<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">slash</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>their stake, essentially deleting their money as a penalty.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 101.43px;"><img id="img-ncS-afbEMPuy5NoPyqXZqAI_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Blockdaemon" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Blockdaemon</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+7</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. How Data is Published and Retrieved<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Because storing large amounts of data directly on a blockchain is very expensive, most systems use a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hybrid Storage Model</strong>:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 52.2031px;"><img id="img-ncS-afbEMPuy5NoPyqXZqAI_3" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="IEEE" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">IEEE</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Publishing (The "Fingerprint")</strong>: Instead of putting the whole "prime number" calculation on the chain, you upload the data to a decentralized storage network like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">IPFS (InterPlanetary File System)</strong>. IPFS gives you a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Content Identifier (CID)</strong>—a unique cryptographic hash or "fingerprint" of that specific data. You then record only that small CID on the blockchain.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Retrieval (The "Permanent Link")</strong>: To get the data back, you use the CID recorded on the blockchain. Because the CID is derived from the data itself, if the data was tampered with even by one digit, the "fingerprint" would no longer match the one stored on the blockchain, and the retrieval would fail.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Permanence</strong>: While a standard blockchain record is permanent, the actual file on IPFS might disappear if no one "pins" (saves) it. To solve this, networks like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Arweave</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>use an endowment fund to pay miners to store data for hundreds of years, creating a "permaweb".</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 89.4062px;"><img id="img-ncS-afbEMPuy5NoPyqXZqAI_19" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="TechTarget" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">TechTarget</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+6</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Summary of Defense<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; animation: var(--fade-in-animation); padding-top: 30px; position: relative; margin-top: 24px;"><div><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>can a 51 attack remove everything, including all previous and current state and make it not working for the ever future</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"></div><div></div></div></div><div><div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div><div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">A<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">51% attack</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>can significantly disrupt a blockchain, but it cannot "remove everything" or permanently break the network beyond repair. While it allows an attacker to rewrite<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">recent</em><span>&nbsp;</span>history, there are hard technical and social limits to what they can achieve.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 65.6641px;"><img id="img-JcW-aefsJLWe5NoPpPqfwQs_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Reddit" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Reddit</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+4</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">What an Attacker Can Do<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Rewrite Recent History</strong>: An attacker can "orphan" recent blocks by creating a longer, secret chain and broadcasting it. This is primarily used for<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">double-spending</strong>—reversing their own transactions to reuse coins.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Censor New Transactions</strong>: They can refuse to include any new transactions in their blocks, effectively freezing the network's current state.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Monopolize Rewards</strong>: They can prevent all other miners or validators from earning rewards, potentially driving them off the network.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 86.7031px;"><img id="img-JcW-aefsJLWe5NoPpPqfwQs_2" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Lightspark" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Lightspark</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+8</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">What an Attacker CANNOT Do<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Change Deep History</strong>: Rewriting very old blocks (e.g., from years ago) is computationally impossible because it would require re-doing the work for every single block since then, faster than the rest of the network.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Steal Your Funds</strong>: An attacker cannot spend coins from your wallet because they lack your<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">private keys</strong>. They can only reverse transactions<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">they</em><span>&nbsp;</span>sent.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Change Protocol Rules</strong>: They cannot create coins out of thin air or change the 21-million cap on Bitcoin, as those rules are enforced by all nodes, not just miners.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 101.695px;"><img id="img-JcW-aefsJLWe5NoPpPqfwQs_18" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Atomic Wallet" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Atomic Wallet</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+7</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Can They Break it "Forever"?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">No. If a 51% attack is sustained, the community has several "nuclear" options to restore the network:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hard Fork</strong>: Developers and users can collectively decide to ignore the attacker's chain and move to a new version of the software that ignores those specific malicious blocks.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Changing the Algorithm</strong>: The network can change its "PoW" algorithm, making the attacker’s expensive specialized hardware (ASICs) instantly useless.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Social Consensus</strong>: Because blockchain is a social agreement as much as a technical one, people will simply stop using and valuing a chain that is clearly under the control of a single malicious entity.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 65.3594px;"><img id="img-JcW-aefsJLWe5NoPpPqfwQs_19" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Reddit" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Reddit</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+6</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Would you like to know how<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">checkpoints</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">finality gadgets</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>are used by some blockchains to prevent deep history from ever being rewritten?<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px;
